name: Crown Land
description: A spatial dataset of all Crown lands in Nova Scotia. Crown lands are all or any part of the land under the administration and control of the Minister of Natural Resources and Renewables as per the Crown Lands Act. The dataset includes land in which the Nova Scotia Department of Natural Resources and Renewables has full or partial interest. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=87 Map service view also available

name: Municipal Property Tax Rates
description: Municipal property taxes are set by the council of each municipality and help fund a variety of municipal services and programs provided by the municipality. There are two different types of tax rates: residential and commercial. All tax rates are applied per $100 of taxable property assessment value. Municipal tax revenue is calculated by multiplying the property assessment value by the applicable tax rate per $100 of assessment value.

name: Crime Statistics - Crime Severity Index
description: The dataset includes crime statistics from law enforcement agencies operating in Nova Scotia. It is based on police-reported incidents of crime reported through the national Uniform Crime Reporting Survey (UCR). Statistics include the Crime Severity Index (CSI), the Violent Crime Severity Index (VCSI), and the Non-violent Crime Severity Index (NVCSI). Data source: Statistics Canada.

name: Ferry Schedules
description: Public Works operates seven ferries. All are links in our provincial highway system and include: Four cable ferries: LaHave - Lunenburg County; Country Harbour, Guysborough County; Little Narrows, Victoria County; Englishtown, Victoria County. Three ocean-going, self-propelled ferries: Tancook Island – Chester; Petit Passage at Digby Neck; Grand Passage at Digby Neck. Content within this dataset relates to the operations schedules for all ferries. Refer to 511 for current availability of service

name: Nova Scotia Government Pay Scales
description: Nova Scotia Government Pay scales based upon effective period start and end dates. Columns include: Start Date, End Date, Pay Plan Type, Pay Plan, Pay Plan Level, Biweekly Pay Rate, Hourly Pay Rate

name: Awarded Public Tenders
description: Contains the awarded vendor & amount for government & public sector tenders by entity, vendor, category, start date, end date and awarded date.

name: Traffic Volumes - Provincial Highway System
description: Traffic volume census on Provincial Highway System

name: Unclaimed Estate or Trust Funds Database
description: An executor, administrator, or trustee may, at any time, and must, after five years of receiving assets of an estate or trust, if the heir-at-law or beneficiary cannot be located for the purposes of distribution of the assets of the estate or trust, apply for an order authorizing the transfer of the assets to the Public Trustee. These assets received by the Public Trustee, pursuant to the direction of the court, are converted into cash and paid to the Minister of Finance and Treasury Board. Uncl

name: Municipality Boundaries
description: This dataset delineates the municipal boundaries for towns and district, county and regional municipalities in Nova Scotia.

name: NS Births and Deaths with Rates and Natural Increase
description: Number of births and deaths by County, with rates (per 1000), showing the the excess of births over deaths and the natural increase rate

name: Nova Scotia Labour Agreements
description: Collective agreements filed with the Minister of Labour, Skills and Immigration pursuant to subsection 4A(12) and Section 46 of the Nova Scotia Trade Union Act.

name: 1:10,000 Nova Scotia Primary Watersheds
description: 1:10,000 watersheds for Nova Scotia. Contains primary watersheds. Also available via GeoNova at: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=82

name: [ARCHIVED] Mortgage Brokers licensed in Nova Scotia

columns.name: Nova Scotia Health Authority Management Zones

description: [ARCHIVED] Amendments to the Mortgage Act came into effect Nov 1, 2021 which created new mortgage license types and a new public register of licensed mortgage brokerages, mortgage lenders and mortgage administrators. Please refer to the new public register for current data: https://beta.novascotia.ca/licensed-mortgage-brokers-and-associate-mortgage-brokers. A mortgage broker is person who functions for a fee as an intermediary between a borrower and lender in securing a mortgage from a lender

name: Hunting and Fishing License Sellers
description: Listing of Hunting and Fishing (including Salmon) License Sellers in Nova Scotia. Includes county, vendor (business) name, address and contact telephone number.

name: African Nova Scotian Family Names by Region

columns.name: African NS Communities in the Region

description: The dataset provides the predominate and traditional family names of African Nova Scotians in 6 regions in Nova Scotia. The regions consist of Halifax Metro, South Shore and Yarmouth and Acadian Shore, Bay of Fundy and Annapolis Valley, Northumberland Shore, Eastern Shore and Cape Breton Island. Within all these regions you find 48+ traditional African Nova Scotian communities. The dataset will also provide the communities you can find in each of the six regions.

name: Nova Scotia Road Network (NSRN)
description: The Nova Scotia Road Network (NSRN) is the authoritative source for road centerlines in the province. This 3D coverage contains addressed roads and non-addressed roads and has several descriptive attributes to define a roadway such as number of lanes, surface type, and road class. The NSRN is used to contribute the Nova Scotia portion of the federal National Road Network (NRN). The NSRN is available in the UTM projection with the following two datums: • UTM/NAD83v3 & CGVD28 horizontal & vertical

name: NS Top Twenty Baby Names - 1920-Current
description: Twenty most popular baby names, by year, for both male and female babies, for Nova Scotia

name: Nova Scotia Control Monuments
description: The Nova Scotia Control Monuments (NSCM) dataset is updated and maintained from ongoing observations on survey monuments. The NSCM indicates the locations of physical survey monuments in Nova Scotia. Information includes the Station Number, Station Name, Station Description, Monument Type, Monument Status, Status Date, Construction Date, GPS suitability and general comments.

columns.name: Manner of Death
columns.description: Manner of death refers to the mode of method of death and is classified as one of five manners: natural; accident; suicide; homicide; undetermined (https://nslegislature.ca/sites/default/files/legc/statutes/fatality%20investigations.pdf). Acute toxicity deaths are not classified as natural in manner and therefore this dataset excludes deaths for which manner was classified as natural. Data are presented for manners suicide and accident.

columns.name: Quarter
columns.description: Quarter in which individual died or was found dead. Q1 = Jan-Mar; Q2 = Apr-Jun; Q3 = Jul-Sep; Q4 Oct-Dec.

columns.name: Place of event
columns.description: Place of toxicity event

columns.description: Describes whether the drugs that contributed to the toxicity death as certified in the cause of death statement belonged to drug class opioids or stimulants (or both or neither)

columns.description: Health zone of residence

columns.name: Others present/aware
columns.description: Describes whether contextual information available suggests others were present or aware that decedent was using substances prior to death

description: This dataset contains frequencies, rates, and proportions that describe drug toxicity deaths in Nova Scotia over time and space and by certain demographic and contextual characteristics. See usage considerations for further details on these data.

name: Nova Scotia Public School Enrolment by RCE/CSAP and School
description: Student enrolment by year, RCE/CSAP and school as well as historical enrolment of school boards.

name: Nova Scotia Government Employee Absenteeism
description: Absences reported by employees by absence date, absence type category, absence type, absence hours, employee type, gender, and age cohort on absence date.

name: Birth Registrations 1864-1877, 1908-1924 (delayed registrations 1830-1924)
description: Civil registration of births in Nova Scotia began in 1864, continued to 1877, then lapsed until 1908, when the province resumed record-keeping. Registrations for 1864-1877 are incomplete. Since 1 October 1908 the records have been maintained without interruption.

name: Nova Scotia Sportfish Angler Records
description: Formerly known as the Nova Scotia Sportfish Registry, this dataset is a list of the current record holders for several popular Nova Scotia sportfish. There are both youth and adult, catch and release and catch and keep and smallmouth bass tournament categories.

name: Specialty Plates
description: Number of specialty plates that are not expired and are attached to a vehicle at a point in time. Specialty plates include Acadian Flag, Buy Local, Conservation, Firefighter, Gaelic, Mi’kmaq, Search and Rescue, Veteran Motorcycle, Veteran, Volunteer Firefighter.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Water Features (Line Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database the Nova Scotia Hydrographic Network (Water Features) is updated and maintained from aerial photography. Lakes, Rivers, Islands and many other features are collected and networked into a seamless relation data base. Toponyms are included in the attribute table for named hydrographic features. Hydrographic feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https:/

name: [ARCHIVED] Access Centres Offices & Hours
description: [ARCHIVED] This dataset has been superseded by Access Centres Office Locations & Hours (https://data.novascotia.ca/Public-Service/Access-Centres-Office-Locations-Hours/f35v-t3mg). Listing of provincial Access Centre office locations and hours of operations.

name: Nova Scotia Freshwater Fish Species Distribution Records
description: List of freshwater fish species recorded from survey data throughout Nova Scotia.

name: Child Care Directory
description: A listing of all currently active daycares and the details of their licence.

name: Death Registrations 1864-1877, 1908-1974; City of Halifax 1890-1908
description: Civil registration of deaths in Nova Scotia began in 1864, continued to 1877, then lapsed until 1908, when the province resumed record-keeping. Registrations for 1864-1877 are incomplete. There are two registers for the city of Halifax 1890-1908. Since 1 October 1908, the records have been maintained without interruption.

name: Marriage Bonds 1763-1864; Marriage Registrations 1864-1949
description: Civil registration of marriages in Nova Scotia began in 1758, with the introduction of procedures for obtaining a marriage license. The license was optional, surviving records are incomplete, and 'calling the banns' remained the preferred procedure for formalizing the marriage ritual. The parallel custom of posting a Marriage Bond, in addition to taking out a license, also dates from this early period. The bond was an optional adjunct which served to indicate the absence of legal impediments to

name: Nova Scotia Civic Address File - Civic Points
description: Province wide dataset of all physical locations which have been assigned a civic number by Municipalities and First Nations Communities. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=70

name: Active Condominium Corporations (Poly Layer)
description: Provincial listing of Active Condominium Corporation in Nova Scotia by County.

name: Public Housing Units - Nova Scotia Seniors
description: This program provides affordable rental housing to seniors (age 58 and older) with low incomes. Rent is determined by your annual income.

name: [ARCHIVED] Land Registration Offices & Hours
description: [ARCHIVED] This dataset has been superseded by Access Centres Office Locations & Hours (https://data.novascotia.ca/Public-Service/Access-Centres-Office-Locations-Hours/f35v-t3mg). Listing of provincial Land Registry office locations and hours of operations

name: "Book of Negroes", 1783
description: The "Book of Negroes" is the single most important document relating to the immigration of African Americans to Nova Scotia following the War of Independence. It provides the names of 3000 black refugees registered on board the vessels in which they sailed from New York to Nova Scotia between 23 April and 30 November 1783.

name: Gaelic Placenames in Nova Scotia
description: Gaelic placenames in Nova Scotia contain placenames Nova Scotia Gaels have for communities in Pictou, Antigonish, Guysborough, Inverness, Victoria, Richmond and the Cape Breton Regional Municipality

name: Licensed Payday Lenders in Nova Scotia
description: List of businesses located in Nova Scotia, licensed to provide payday loans

name: Nova Scotia Co-operatives
description: List of co-operatives that are registered at the Registry of Joint Stock Companies as of September 25, 2025. Information includes: registry identification number, co-operative name, year of incorporation, mailing address. This list also identifies whether a co-operative is incorporated as a non-profit or for-profit association, and the co-op type (for example, housing, investment, retail consumer products, services, agriculture, craft products, fish products, forest products, or worker-labour).

name: Nova Scotia Provincial Ambient Fine Particulate Matter (PM2.5) Hourly Data Halifax BAM/T640
description: Hourly ambient fine particulate matter (PM2.5) data in micrograms per cubic metre from provincial ambient air quality monitoring stations across Nova Scotia up to the end of 2024. Since the beginning of 2018, this station is co-located with the "Halifax Johnston" gas analyzers (NOx, NO2, NO, SO2, O3 & CO)

name: NS Weather Station Data
description: Weather data collected from Public Work's weather stations located around the Province.

name: Electric Vehicle Registration Data
description: The number of Electric Vehicles registered in Nova Scotia with an active licence plate attached. This data breaks down the number of electric vehicles by vehicle model year, vehicle make and County of Nova Scotia in which the licence plate owner resides.

name: Recognized Private Schools Granting the Nova Scotia High School Leaving Certificate

columns.name: Renewal Date of Designation or Recognition

name: Deer Management Zones
description: Nova Scotia is divided in 12 deer management zones and deer hunting regulations may vary by zone. When accuracy is needed, consult the official legal wording of the Wildlife Act and its Regulations. Laws are subject to change at any time and are available online. Links to wildlife related acts can be found here: https://novascotia.ca/natr/wildlife/laws/actsregs.asp. For a summary of the hunting and furharvesting regulations in Nova Scotia, see: https://novascotia.ca/natr/hunt/pdf/hunting-and-fur

name: The Nova Scotia Protected Areas System
description: These lands help preserve Nova Scotia’s natural values through a blend of legislation, ownership and management. Included here are: National Parks, National Wildlife Areas, Provincial Wilderness Areas, Provincial Nature Reserves, selected Provincial Parks and selected land trust properties and easements. This combination of federal, provincial and private lands contributes to both provincial and national land conservation and biodiversity goals.

name: Co-operatives Financial and Operating Summary
description: Co-operatives are required to report financial and operational information to the Co-operatives Branch on an annual basis. This report includes aggregate amounts for each of the past ten years including the number of co-operatives registered at the Registry of Joint Stock Companies, reports received, new Incorporations, co-operatives struck from the Registry, total income, expenses, net income, assets, liabilities, equity, employees (full and part time), members, percentage and number of co-oper

name: Nova Scotia Topographic Database - Digital Terrain Model (DTM)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), DTM is a vector data set of spot elevations collected from aerial photography. An elevation point is collected at ground level approximately every eighty meters in an irregular grid pattern. These spot elevations, or mass points, can be used to create profiles of the ground or Digital Elevation Models (DEM). Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=37 Map Servic

name: Nova Scotia Marine Aquaculture Leases
description: File contains all issued marine aquaculture leases along the coast of Nova Scotia. Fisheries and Aquaculture also provides a mapping tool for this data at: Nova Scotia Aquaculture & Rockweed Map Viewer

name: Graduation Rates
description: 2016-17 Graduation Information by board, 2016-17 Graduation Information by gender, and Historical Graduation Rates. Note: Graduation Rate is the percentage of the number of students receiving a High School Graduation Diploma compared to the number of students in grade 9 three years earlier.

name: Nova Scotia Fish Hatchery Stocking Records
description: Fish stocking records from Nova Scotia fish hatcheries. Includes trout and salmon stocking, size, number, location, stock and growth stage. Records contain both fall and spring distributions.

name: Crime Statistics - Incidents and rates for selected offences
description: The dataset includes incident-based crime statistics at the provincial and police service level. Statistics include the number of incidents of offences and rates per 100,000 population for the following offences: Homicide, Attempted murder , Sexual assault (Levels 1, 2 and 3), Total sexual violations against children, Assault (Levels 1, 2 and 3), Total robbery, Total breaking and entering, Total theft of motor vehicle, Total impaired driving, Total drug violations, Possession (cannabis), Possess

name: Nova Scotia Provincial Ambient Ground-level Ozone (O3) Hourly Data Halifax Johnston
description: Hourly ambient ground-level ozone (O3) data in parts per billion from provincial ambient air quality monitoring stations across Nova Scotia up to the end of 2024. This air quality station was established to replace the "Halifax Vogue" air quality monitoring station which ceased monitoring at the end of 2017. The Halifax Vogue station was less than 1 block away from the new "Halifax Johnston" station.

name: Nova Scotia Minimum Wage
description: Provincial regulations establish the province’s minimum wage. Minimum wage is the lowest legal wage employers can pay their employees. This dataset includes hourly minimum wage rates in Nova Scotia dating back to 1965; weekly rates that were set between 1958 and before February 20, 1965 are not included. Employers, employees, and the public request minimum wage data, including in relation to historical rates - this dataset shows the changes overtime.

Location Quotient based on Business Locations

description: The dataset provides information on industry specialization, industry structure and number of business locations for Nova Scotia’s Functional Economic Regions. The data fields include: Functional Economic Region Name, Month and Year; Category (e.g., Farming); Type (Traded or Local); North American Industry Classification (NAICS) - 6-digits; Location Quotient based on Business Locations; and Employment Size Categories for Business Locations. Fields may be added to or change over time.

name: Old Growth Forest Policy Layer

columns.name: SELMETHTXT
columns.description: Text description of SELMETHOD values.

columns.name: SELMETHOD
columns.description: Indicates which method was used in adding stands to the layer. Stands inside protected areas that were added using data from the Predictive Ecosystem Map and forest inventory are given a value of 2. All other stands added as either old growth or restoration opportunities are assigned a value of 1.

columns.name: OLDGROWTXT
columns.description: Text description of Old_Growth values.

columns.name: Old_Growth
columns.description: Indicates whether a stand has been confirmed as old growth forest. Stands outside of protected areas that have been confirmed as old growth forest using NRR’s assessment protocol, are assigned a value of 1. Stands outside of protected areas that are restoration opportunities or whose old growth status is unknown have a value of 0. All stands inside protected areas are given a value of 0.

columns.name: HECTARES
columns.description: Polygon area in hectares.

description: Forest stands identified under the Nova Scotia Old Growth Forest Policy.

name: Uniform Assessment
description: One measure of a municipality's financial health is its own-source revenue base as represented by the total uniform assessment. Uniform Assessment is the value of a municipality’s taxable property assessment plus the capitalized value of payments made to municipalities in lieu of taxes (i.e., grant-in-lieu).

name: Census, Assessment and Poll Tax Records 1767-1838
description: The database presented here brings together an assortment of early census, property assessment, and poll tax records, providing a total of 69,807 personal names. None of the census, assessment or poll tax records presented here is complete for the province - even if they claimed to be so at the time. None of the individual returns is complete internally either; people and households were overlooked, and sections of communities were undoubtedly missed.

name: Sea-level Rise (outdated: CMIP3/2011)
description: *Please note that this data is now outdated, as climate modeling methods have improved since CMIP3. Users seeking updated sea level rise projections for Nova Scotia can find this data at: https://data.novascotia.ca/Nature-and-Environment/NS-Sea-Level-Rise-Projections-CMIP5-/t5sw-u3sr Sea-level rise projections for regions of Nova Scotia. The data includes projections for the following time periods: 2025, 2055, 2085 and 2100.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Roads, Trails and Rails (Break Line Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the roads and railroads theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography and collected in the field with mobile GPS, Field inspections verify road way types such as surface and number of lanes. Road feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=15 Map servi

name: Nova Scotia Topographic Database - Land Cover (Line Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the land cover theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography. Land cover is limited to treed areas over 3600m2 in size and over two metres in height. Orchards, nurseries, tree lines, and single landmark trees are also included. Land Cover feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/Downloa

name: Nova Scotia Lake Chemistry Data

The following weblink connects to a Department of Environment and Climate Change web map that includes the locations of the monitored lakes within the province and an alternative m

description: Financial Condition Indicators are produced by the Department which provide a general picture of a municipality's financial condition. The source of information for this dataset is derived from the Financial Information Return (FIR) and the audited financial statements of Nova Scotia’s municipalities. DMA calculates the dataset and reviews accuracy with municipalities.

name: Nova Scotia Community Clusters
description: Nova Scotia Community Clusters for health service planning and research. Includes the following data fields: GIS_UID, GIS_SELECTED, GIS_HIDDEN, GIS_LENGTH, GIS_AREA, Cluster, ClusterID

name: Number of People Represented by Nominee Certificates
description: The Department of Labour, Skills, and Immigration (LSI) issues nomination certificates to prospective immigrants who meet a labour market need and who will make a contribution to Nova Scotia’s economy. Nominees then apply to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada for a permanent resident visa. Certificates are issued in particular streams of the Nominee Program, and each stream has different eligibility criteria. Over time, streams may close as new streams are developed. This dataset provi

name: Boil Water Advisories
description: This dataset includes the following information for public registered and municipal drinking water supplies: Site Name, County, Date Boil Advisory Issued, Date Boil Advisory Removed, Facility Type (Registered or Municipal), Length of Advisory in Days, Reason for the Report. This dataset includes boil water advisories from 2006 - current.

name: Nova Scotia GeoNAMES
description: The Nova Scotia GeoNAMES dataset contains all current approved and offical geographic names for the Province of Nova Scotia. The named features in the dataset include Populated Places, Administrative Areas, Water and Terrain Features, Vegetation Areas, some Manmade Features, and Undersea Features. Geographic names in the Province of Nova Scotia are approved by the province's representative on the Geographical Names Board of Canada.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Buildings (Point Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the buildings theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography. Buildings over 30m (one side) are collected as polygons, all others as points. Selected buildings are inspected in the field or interpreted from aerial photography and classified according to use such as hospitals, schools, police stations or community centres. Building feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table.

name: Nomination Certificates Issued
description: The Department of Labour, Skills and Immigration (LSI) issues nomination certificates to prospective immigrants who fulfil a labour market need and who will make a contribution to Nova Scotia’s economy. Nominees then apply to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada for a permanent resident visa. The maximum number of certificates that LSI may issue annually through the Nova Scotia Nominee Program is determined by the federal government.

name: Nova Scotia Assessment Results by Year and Board - Reading and Writing in Grade 8
description: Provides assessment results for Reading (scaled score and performance level) and Writing (performance level for four components of writing) for each year assessment has been administered. Consult plans.ednet.ns.ca for more information on the assessments.

name: Surplus Crown Property Auction Total Gross (Agent)
description: This category covers those items of surplus, not including Real Property, that have a minor monetary value but, after investigation by departmental supply technicians, are deemed to have no known future use within the government. In addition, it would be determined by surplus inventory staff that the item could not economically be refurbished.

name: Schools with French Programs

columns.name: Nova Scotia Health Authority Management Zones

description: List of schools offering French programs.

name: Surplus Crown Property Disposal - Real Property
description: "This category covers items of surplus in the Real Property classification. Real Property, in general, is considered land or buildings that are deemed surplus to the needs of the various government departments responsible for their management. "

name: Village Boundaries
description: This dataset delineates the village boundaries for Nova Scotia.

name: Smallmouth Bass and Chain Pickerel Tournaments 2019
description: A list of all smallmouth bass and chain pickerel tournaments permitted under the Competitive Fishing Policy

name: Surface Water Quality Monitoring Network Grab Sample Water Quality Data
description: The Nova Scotia Automated Surface Water Quality Monitoring Network was established in 2002 to assess water quality in surface waters across the province at five stations. In 2019 the station list was modified to reduce overlap with Environment and Climate Change Canada surface water quality monitoring stations. The results are used to help manage water resources, determine baseline water quality in lakes and watercourses throughout the province, evaluate the impact of human activities on surface

name: Crime Statistics - Incidents and rates by offence category
description: The dataset includes incident-based crime statistics at the provincial and police service level . Statistics include the number of incidents of offences and rates per 100,000 population for Criminal Code violations (excluding traffic), violent Criminal Code violations, property crime violations, and other Criminal Code violations. Data source: Statistics Canada.

name: Rebates for New and Used Electric Vehicles in Nova Scotia
description: The province is strengthening its commitment to address climate change and grow the economy with its new Electric Vehicle Rebate program. The rebate program gives Nova Scotians of varying income levels to take part in the shift to clean transportation. The Clean Foundation administers the point-of-sale rebate program through auto dealers and bicycle retailers.

name: [ARCHIVED] Nova Scotia Communities Engaged in Age-Friendly Community Planning
description: [ARCHIVED] The “Nova Scotia Communities Engaged in Age-Friendly Community Planning” dataset is no longer being updated. This dataset has been superseded by Age-Friendly Communities Grant Funding Recipients. List of communities across Nova Scotia that are engaged in Age-Friendly Community planning.

name: Nova Scotia Assessment Results by Year and Board - Mathematics in Grade 4
description: Provides assessment results for Mathematics (scaled score and performance level) for each year assessment has been administered. Consult plans.ednet.ns.ca for more information on the assessments.

name: NS Climate Change Data (outdated: CMIP3/2011)
description: *Please note that this data is now outdated, as climate modeling methods have improved since CMIP3. Users seeking updated climate projections for Nova Scotia can find this data at: https://data.novascotia.ca/Nature-and-Environment/NS-Climate-Change-Projections-CMIP5-/r7d9-j7wx Climate data for regions of Nova Scotia. Each region has historical data for a 30 year baseline period (1961 – 90) and future projections out to the end of the century (2100).

name: Nova Scotia Public School Contact Information
description: Public school mailing address, civic address, telephone, fax

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Structures (Poly Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the structures theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography, and verified with field inspections. Chimneys, Incinerators, Silos and Weigh Scales are all examples of what is found in this theme. Structure feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=16

name: Nova Scotia Provincial Ambient Air Quality Monitoring Stations

description: Nova Scotia provincial ambient air quality monitoring stations, including locations and other attributes.

name: Ecological Land Classification (ELC) - 2015
description: The Ecological Land Classification (ELC version 2015) for Nova Scotia provides a hierarchical mapping of the province's forest ecosystems into ecosections, ecodistricts and ecoregions. It includes interpretation of the dominant natural disturbance regimes and potential climax forests at the ecosection level.

name: Trout Derby Schedule
description: A list of all trout derbies permitted under the Competitive Fishing Policy

name: Alcohol, Gaming, Fuel & Tobacco Disciplinary Notices
description: Summary of recent disciplinary action and related information regarding non-compliant matters that have been brought before the Executive Director of Alcohol, Gaming, Fuel and Tobacco Division resulting in issuance of a Notice of Proposed Action. Sanctions for similar infractions may vary in length according to the specifics of each case.

name: Film and Television Production Incentive Fund Activity
description: The Nova Scotia Film & Television Production Incentive Fund provides funding for screen-based content creation. To be eligible, organizations must have a permanent establishment in the province and be engaged primarily in the creation of film/video productions for public viewing.

name: Agricultural Land Protection
description: This dataset provides information about how agricultural land is being protected through municipal land use planning. Each municipality is listed along with the level of agricultural land protection found within municipal land use plans. Agricultural protection is described as either exclusive zoning for agricultural land, agriculture identified as permitted use, or no planning for agriculture. Assessment of Agricultural Protection Municipal planning strategies and land-use by-laws were assessed

name: Nova Scotia Examination Results by year and board - Mathematics 10
description: Provides examination results for Mathematics (scaled score and performance level) for each year examination has been administered. Consult plans.ednet.ns.ca for more information on the examinations.

name: Family Resource Program
description: Family Resource Programs provide programs and services for parents and families to support the well-being and development of their children. These include parenting programs, family support, child development activities, practical supports, basic needs/resources, information, referrals, etc.

name: Skilled Trades Centres
description: A listing of public schools offering Skilled Trades programming. Includes RCE/board, school, civic address and mailing address.

name: Tancook Ferry Schedule
description: Public Works operates seven ferries. This data covers the ferry schedule for the Tancook Island ferry. Refer to 511 for current availability of service: http://511.gov.ns.ca/en/map/

name: Average Annual Numbers and Rates of Suicide Fatalities in NS by age group and sex
description: Average Annual Numbers and Rates of Suicide Fatalities in NS by age group and sex, time periods: 2010-2012, 2013-2015, 2016-2018, 2019-2021.

name: Nova Scotia Civic Address File - Addressable Roads
description: The Civic Addressable Roads dataset is a road network that contains road geometry only where civic addressing exists. The dataset contains civic address information from the Nova Scotia Civic Address File.

name: Halifax County Water Quality Data

columns.description: Dissolved oxygen measurement, in units of percent saturation

columns.name: qc_flag_sensor_depth_measured_m
columns.description: Quality control flag for measured sensor depth. Maximum flag value from three automated quality control tests: grossrange,  spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_salinity_psu
columns.description: Quality control flag for salinity. Maximum flag value from three automated quality control tests: grossrange,  spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: sensor_depth_measured_m
columns.description: Depth of the sensor, as measured by the sensor in metres

columns.name: salinity_psu
columns.description: Salinity measurement, in practical salinity units

columns.name: dissolved_oxygen_uncorrected_mg_per_l
columns.description: Dissolved oxygen measurement, in units of mg/L, without applying a salinity correction factor

columns.name: sensor_depth_at_low_tide_m
columns.description: Estimated depth of the sensor at low tide, in metres, based on its position in the sensor string configuration

columns.name: timestamp_utc
columns.description: Timestamp at which a measurement was made, in UTC. The formatting is "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"

columns.name: sensor_serial_number
columns.description: Unique identifying number for the sensor that recorded the measurement

columns.name: sensor_type
columns.description: Model of the sensor that recorded the measurement

columns.name: deployment_range
columns.description: String describing the instrument deployment and retrieval dates. The format is "YYYY-MMM-DD to YYYY-MMM-DD"

columns.name: longitude
columns.description: Longitude of the deployment (decimal degrees)

columns.name: latitude
columns.description: Latitude of the deployment (decimal degrees)

columns.name: station
columns.description: Name of the location for this deployment. Deployments at the same station must be within 500 m of defined station coordinates.

columns.name: waterbody
columns.description: Waterbody in which the station for this deployment is located

columns.name: qc_flag_temperature_degree_c
columns.description: Quality control flag for temperature. Maximum flag value from three automated quality control tests: grossrange,  spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_dissolved_oxygen_uncorrected_mg_per_l
columns.description: Quality control flag for dissolved oxygen (mg/L). Maximum flag value from four automated quality control tests: grossrange, climatology, spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_dissolved_oxygen_percent_saturation
columns.description: Quality control flag for dissolved oxygen (percent saturation). Maximum flag value from four automated quality control tests: grossrange, climatology, spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: temperature_degree_c
columns.description: Temperature measurement, in degrees Celsius

columns.name: depth_crosscheck_flag
columns.description: Quality control flag for sensor depth at low tide. Flag values can be "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail". The same flag is applied to the whole deployment.

columns.name: string_configuration
columns.description: Configuration in which the sensors were deployed. This can be one of six entries: “sub-surface buoy”, “surface buoy”, “attached to gear”, “attached to fixed structure”, “floating dock”, or “unknown”

The data published includes the file number, the Grant recipient, the applicant, the Company, the Project Name, the Commodity and the Amount awarded.

name: [ARCHIVED] Select Nova Scotia
description: [ARCHIVED] The Select Nova Scotia program has ended and the data contained in this dataset is no longer being collected. Select Nova Scotia is a program developed by the Province of Nova Scotia to encourage Nova Scotians to shop and buy locally. Select Nova Scotia promotions and campaigns and the web site raise consumer awareness about Nova Scotia agri-food and seafood products, and other locally made goods.

name: Settlement Service Provider Directory
description: A directory and map of settlement service providers in Nova Scotia. Provides contact information for the organizations (Mailing and physical addresses, phone numbers) as well as a list of the types of services available through each service provider (Assessment and Orientation, Language, Employment and Business, Youth and Young Adult, Recreation and Socialization, Francophone, Distance/Multiple Locations, and Other).

description: Under the Municipal Government Act, the Minister has the authority to prescribe the type of information to be provided by a municipality to the Minister [s.451 (1(b)]. In Nova Scotia, the municipality is required to submit financial information to the Department of Municipal Affairs (DMA) through the standard Financial Information Return (FIR). The Province then compiles an Annual Municipal Statistics Report based on the data municipalities provide.

name: Nova Scotia Paid Holidays and Retail Closing Days
description: Provides dates of paid holidays and designated retail closing days in Nova Scotia to be used as a reference for employers and employees.

name: Ecological Land Classification (ELC) – 2007 (Historical)
description: For most recent Ecological Land Classification data, see: https://data.novascotia.ca/d/q6zd-39t3 The Ecological Land Classification (ELC) for Nova Scotia provides a hierarchical mapping of the province's forest ecosystems into ecosections, ecodistricts and ecoregions. It includes interpretation of the dominant natural disturbance regimes and potential climax forests at the ecosection level.

name: Municipal Financial Capacity Grant and Transitional Support Program
description: The Municipal Financial Capacity Grant (MFCG) is an unconditional grant to support municipalities and towns in providing services to Nova Scotians. MFCG is legislated under the Municipal Grants Act, with the specific formula calculating the grant detailed in the accompanying regulations. The formula, modernized in 2024-25, attempts to measure a municipality’s cost to deliver a set of municipal services against its ability to pay for those services, when compared to similar municipalities. Under

name: An Acadian Parish Remembered: The Registers of St. Jean-Baptiste, Annapolis Royal, 1702-1755
description: A fully searchable database containing information for 3551 baptisms, marriages and burials recorded in the parish registers of St.-Jean-Baptiste, Annapolis Royal, 1702-1755. These two volumes are Nova Scotia's earliest surviving church registers, and a tangible link to the last generations of Acadian French living at Annapolis before the Deportation. Transcription and translation of the entries into both official languages is Nova Scotia Archives 's contribution to the Acadie 2003-2005 Celebrat

name: Women's Resources and Services in Nova Scotia

Friends, family members, service providers, or other support people may also find these resources useful.

name: Lunenburg County Current Data

columns.name: waterbody
columns.description: waterbody in which the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: qc_flag_sensor_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: Quality control flag for sensor depth below surface. This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water speed (m/s). This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: station
columns.description: specific area where ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: bin_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: distance from the surface to the centre of the measurement bin (metres). Calculated as sensor_depth_below_surface_m + sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m - bin_height_above_sea_floor_m. See “Nova Scotia Current Data: Deployment Information” dataset (https://data.novascotia.ca/d/rs2e-ekha) for sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m.

columns.name: bin_height_above_sea_floor_m
columns.description: distance from the surface to the centre of the measurement bin (metres). Calculated as sensor_depth_below_surface_m + sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m - bin_height_above_sea_floor_m. See “Nova Scotia Current Data: Deployment Information” dataset (https://data.novascotia.ca/d/rs2e-ekha) for sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m.

columns.name: sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: water speed for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (m / s).

columns.name: qc_flag_sea_water_to_direction_degree
columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water direction. This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: deployment_id
columns.description: unique identifier for ADCP deployment. IDs are assigned incrementally by deployment date within a county. A skipped ID (e.g., consecutive IDs are XY001 and XY003) indicates that an ADCP was deployed but no useable current data were recovered (e.g., the battery failed).

columns.name: sensor_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: the depth of the ADCP below the surface (metres), measured by the ADCP sensor. Changes with the tide.

columns.name: timestamp_utc
columns.description: timestamp of the start of the ensemble (in UTC). Current speed and direction recorded at each timestamp are an average of many measurements recoded during the ensemble. See the “Nova Scotia Current Data Deployment Information” dataset for ensemble details for each deployment.

columns.name: sea_water_to_direction_degree
columns.description: direction of water flow for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (degrees). Measured travelling away from the ADCP sensor relative to True North (a reading of 90 degrees describes current travelling from west to east).

name: Geography - Districts
description: A geographic boundary of Public Work's districts, which are used in allocating operational resources. Each district is managed by a District Director. Each district is divided into 3-4 management areas, and each management area is divided into 2-7 operational zones.

name: Municipal Affairs Funding Programs
description: The Department of Municipal Affairs and Housing administers a variety of programs that provide funding to municipalities and not-for-profit community groups. This dataset includes a list of the funding programs that are administered by Department of Municipal Affairs and Housing. Information include: Funding Program, Area, Year, Actual Funding Amount.

name: Nova Scotia Examination Results by year and board - English 10
description: Provides examination results for Reading (scaled score and performance level) and Writing (performance level for four components of writing) for each year examination has been administered. Consult plans.ednet.ns.ca for more information on the examinations.

name: Winter Levels of Service (LOS)
description: Winter LOS is a GIS file that depicts the Department of Public Work's Winter Maintenance Levels of Service on all Department of Public Works' owned roadways. The definitions associated with Winter Levels of Service can be found at http://novascotia.ca/tran/winter/WinterMaintenanceStandards.pdf

name: Nova Scotia Landbased Aquaculture Licenses

Fisheries and Aquaculture also provides a mapping tool for this data at: http://novascotia.ca/fish/aquaculture/site-mapping-tool/

name: Nominee Certificates Issued by Stream
description: The Department of Labour, Skills and Immigration (LSI) issues nomination certificates to prospective immigrants who meet a labour market need and who will make a contribution to Nova Scotia’s economy. Nominees then apply to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada for a permanent resident visa. Certificates are issued in particular streams of the Nominee Program, and each stream has different eligibility criteria. Over time, streams may close as new streams are developed. description: This category covers items of surplus, not including Real Property, that were publicly tendered for purchase and removal from specific locations throughout the Province.

name: Boundary File of Nova Scotia’s Self-contained Labour Areas (2016)
description: A geographic shape file of Nova Scotia’s Functional Economic Regions. The most widely accepted approach to identifying functional economic regions is based on self-contained labour areas (SLAs) - a group of two or more Census Subdivisions (CSDs) where at least 75% of the workers both live and work in the area. Using Census data, Statistics Canada has identified Census Subdivisions (CSDs) that can be grouped together into SLAs based on daily commuting flows. Department of Business has mapped thes

name: Age of Nominees at Time of Application
description: The Department of Labour, Skills and Immigration (LSI) issues nomination certificates to prospective immigrants who meet a labour market need and who will make a contribution to Nova Scotia’s economy. Nominees then apply to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada for a permanent resident visa. Nominees must generally be between the ages of 21 to 55 at time of application, but the LSI reserves the right to waive this criteria in exceptional circumstances. This dataset provides the number of

name: Crime Statistics - Youth Charged and Not Charged by Offence Category (provincial level)
description: The dataset includes incident-based crime statistics at the provincial level. Statistics included in the dataset are the number of youth charged or not charged, and rates per 100,000 youth population for total Criminal Code violations (excluding traffic), total violent Criminal Code violations, total property crime violations, and total other Criminal Code violations. Data source: Statistics Canada.

name: 1:10,000 Nova Scotia Sub-Tertiary Watersheds
description: 1:10,000 watersheds for Nova Scotia. Contains sub-tertiary watersheds. Also available via GeoNova at: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=82

name: Big Brothers Big Sisters
description: Big Brothers Big Sisters provides children and youth with high quality, professionally supported volunteer mentoring programs. This includes the pairing of children and youth to adult role models who serve as a mentor and a friend to talk to and share the experiences of growing up with. Big Brothers Big Sisters also provides children and youth with opportunities to participate in group programs, both in school and outside of school.

name: Municipal Solid Waste Curbside Collection Tonnage
description: Per service unit (residential dwelling & small businesses provided curbside collection) tonnages for waste, recycling, organics collection

name: Surplus Crown Property: Surplus Disposal - Public School Boards
description: This category covers surplus furniture and equipment that was transferred to public schools throughout the Province.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Landforms (Line Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the landforms theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography. Contours, spot elevations, Cut/fill locations along roadways, as well as cliffs and embankments are collected. Landform feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=36758 Map service view also

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Buildings (Poly Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the buildings theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography. Buildings over 30m (one side) are collected as polygons, all others as points. Selected buildings are inspected in the field or interpreted from aerial photography and classified according to use such as hospitals, schools, police stations or community centres. Building feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic DataBase - Roads, Trails and Rails (Line Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the roads and railroads theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography and collected in the field with mobile GPS, Field inspections verify road way types such as surface and number of lanes. Road feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=15 Map servi

name: Community Economic Development Fund
description: The number of Community Economic Development applications received, declined, and approved.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic Database - Land Cover (Poly)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the land cover theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography. Land cover is limited to treed areas over 3600m2 in size and over two metres in height. Orchards, nurseries, tree lines, and single landmark trees are also included. Land Cover feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/Downloa

name: Nova Scotia Provincial Ambient Carbon Monoxide (CO) Hourly Data Halifax Johnston
description: Hourly ambient carbon monoxide (CO) data in parts per million from provincial ambient air quality monitoring stations across Nova Scotia up to the end of 2024. This air quality station was established to replace the "Halifax Vogue" air quality monitoring station which ceased monitoring at the end of 2017. The Halifax Vogue station was less than 1 block away from the new "Halifax Johnston" station.

name: Municipal Contribution To Roads
description: According to the Provincial Municipal Service Exchange Agreement, municipalities are required to pay the province for the cost of maintaining of the local roads constructed pre-April 1, 1995. The fees paid by municipalities started at $3,500 per kilometer and is adjusted annually by the CPI for Canada.

name: Nova Scotia Public Library Circulation Counts
description: Monthly counts of items checked out and renewed at Nova Scotia Public Libraries.

name: Nova Scotia Assessment Results by Year and Board - Lecture et écriture en 6e année
description: Provides assessment results for Reading (scaled score and performance level) and Writing (performance level for four components of writing) for each year assessment has been administered. Consult plans.ednet.ns.ca for more information on the assessments.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic Database - Structures (Line Layer)
description: Part of the Nova Scotia Topographic Database (NSTDB), the structures theme layer is updated and maintained from aerial photography, and verified with field inspections. Chimneys, Incinerators, Silos and Weigh Scales are all examples of what is found in this theme. Structure feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. Data download also available via GeoNova: https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/WSF_DDS/DDS.svc/DownloadFile?tkey=fhrTtdnDvfytwLz6&id=16

name: University Enrolment

columns.name: StudentOrigin
columns.description: Derived text based variable which categorizes those reporting Nova Scotia, as "Nova Scotian" students, those reporting any Canadian province and 'Outside of Canada' as province of residence as "Out of Province" students and those reporting international as "International" students. These categories correspond with tuition fees charged by institutions to different types of students.

columns.name: ProvinceOfResidence
columns.description: Text based variable that describes the Province or place of residence at the student's time of admissions to the unviersity. Categories include: British Colombia; Alberta, Northwest Territories, Yukon, Nunuvut, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec, New Brunswick, PEI, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Outside of Canada (used for Canadian citizens/permanent residents applying from outside of Canada such as those living as expats in another country but deemed residents for taxation purposes) and International (category reservered for non-Canadian citizens/permananent residents who would be considered international students when attending a post-secondary institution in Canada).

columns.name: ProgramType
columns.description: Text based variable that classifies the program type based on a combination of factors such as the general purpose of the program, the type of instruction offered (educational content); and the expected outcome of the program.

columns.name: ReportYear
columns.description: Date variable indicates the academic year of the reporting, expressed as YYYY-YYYY. Enrolment data are based on PSIS's reporting cycles which start on the day after the end of the insitution's previous year's winter session (or academic year if they did not have a winter session), including the time allocated for exams.  If the institution has no activity during the summer, the University would use September 1st as the start of their report cycle if their academic year ends on August 31st. Fo many university's the end of Winter session typically falls between April 15-30 each year. See File ID; Element 1005 in the PSIS Record Layout file. https://www.mphec.ca/media/223264/MPHEC_PSIS_Record_Layout_MPHEC_2023.pdf

columns.name: RegistrationStatus
columns.description: Boolean text based variable which indicates the registration status of the student as of December 1st during the academic reporting year; expressed as either "Full-time" or "Part-time". Full-time status is defined as the registration status of students who are enrolled in credit courses and/or program of study that are taking at least 60% of the normal course work associated with a program year. Part-time refers to the registration status of students wo are taking less than 60% of the normal course work associated with a program year. If the student is also a graduate student, part-time can also include the following types of students: 1) Students who have completed their required course work and are writing their thesis; and 2) Students who have not completed their course work and are presently taking courses and/or working on their thesis.

columns.name: Enrolment
columns.description: Numeric variable which indicates the total number of students enrolled in the academic year, at the instituion as per registration status, program type, credential type, level of study, major field of study, province of residence and student origin; expressed as a whole number.

columns.name: MajorFieldOfStudy
columns.description: Text based variable that describes the major field of study the program corresponds to. Categories include: Agriculture, natural resources, and conservation; Architecture, engineering and related technologies; Busines, management and public administration; Education; General Arts; General Science; Health and related fields; Humanities (exlcudes general arts); Mathematics, computer and information sciences; Not applicable; Other; Physical and life sciences (excludes general science); Social and behavioral sciences and law; Visual and performing arts.

columns.name: Institution
columns.description: String text variable which indicates the name of the University.

columns.name: CredentialType
columns.description: Text based variable that describes the type of credential that a student earns upon graduation based on Statistic Canada's Classification of Program Credentials. Categories include: Attestation and other short program credentials; Certificate; Credit program with no credential (common first year or similar pre-requisite); Degree; Diploma; Not applicable.

columns.name: LevelOfStudy
columns.description: Boolean text based variable which indicates whether the program and credential is an Undergraduate or Graduate level program.

description: The following dataset contains aggregate enrolment counts by institution, registration status, program type, credential type, level of study, Major field of study, Residence (by Province/International), and student origin

name: Parenting Journey Program

columns.name: Nova Scotia Health Authority Management Zones

description: Parenting Journey is a home visitation program that provides support for families experiencing complex social, emotional and familial challenges that may impact overall family functioning, parent-child relationships and the well-being and development of children/youth ages 0 to 18 plus. The Parenting Journey Program is intended to serve families with the fewest resources, facing multiple challenges, and experiencing barriers to accessing supports.

name: Nova Scotia Lake Survey Lakes Locations
description: A shapefile of freshwater water bodies hat have been sampled as part of the Nova Scotia Lake Survey. The Nova Scotia Lake Survey program is a partnership initiative between Nova Scotia Environment (NSE) and Nova Scotia Fisheries and Aquaculture (NSDFA) to inventory lakes throughout the province determining baseline water quality, in support of both sport fisheries and water resource management areas. The following weblink connects to a Nova Scotia Environment web map that includes the locations

name: NS Births by Weight and Gestational Age
description: Number of births by birth weight in grams and gestational age in weeks for all of Nova Scotia.

name: Farm Land Grant
description: The Municipal Government Act authorizes the Minister to pay grants to municipalities for farm property exempt from taxation based on a formula outlined in the Act. The farm acreage for each municipality is provided by the Property Valuation Services Corporation.

name: Invest Nova Scotia Incubation Resident Companies
description: Invest Nova Scotia is Nova Scotia’s business development agency that works to promote economic growth and community economic development in the province by enabling business, innovation, sustainability and entrepreneurship. Early stage deep technology start-ups are a key focus area for Invest Nova Scotia. They are supported through venture capital, incubation facilities, expert advice, and other acceleration initiatives. The Bays at Invest Nova Scotia in Dartmouth targets companies in clean tech

name: Approved CAEC Testing Locations in NS
description: Dataset provides location information on approved CAEC testing locations in Nova Scotia by providing the campus, street address, city/town and geographic co-ordinates of testing sites.

name: Index for Regional "Innovation-Driven Entrepreneurship" (IDE)
description: The dataset provides indicators for assessing “innovation-driven entrepreneurship’ in regional ecosystems (iEcosystems) based on Nova Scotia’s Functional Economic Regions. Each indicator includes a score of 1 to 10 on how a region performs against other regions across Canada. The data fields include: Index Year, Functional Economic Region Name, Indicator, and Score. Indicators are subject to change over time.

description: Asthma prevalence data is from the Canadian Chronic Disease Surveillance System (CCDSS). Asthma prevalence in Nova Scotia. Includes the following data fields: Year, Management Zone, Sex, Age Group, Population, Asthma Prevalence Count, Crude Prevalence Rate %

name: Municipal Land Use Planning Comprehensiveness
description: This dataset provides information about the extent of municipal land use planning in Nova Scotia. Each municipality is listed along with the type of its land use planning described as either comprehensive for entire municipality, comprehensive for local areas only, comprehensive for towns, or single issue. It also lists the date of the municipal land use plans described. A comprehensive strategy is one that address all major areas of concern, for example: residential, commercial, industrial, rec

name: Surplus Crown Property: Scrap Waste
description: This category encompasses items of surplus, not including Real Property, that have been determined to be, upon inspection, of no value to government and are in such condition that refurbishing is uneconomical. Any reusable or recyclable components are removed prior to disposal. The use of this option may also be based on environmental or safety considerations such as the obsolescence of the item; i.e., baby crib, mattresses, medical equipment, et cetera.

name: Connect2
description: One of the action items of the Sustainable Transportation Strategy, was to develop a grant program focused on providing funds to communities for sustainable transportation related projects. The grant program, Nova Scotia Moves was launched in 2012/2013 and went on for 2 fiscal years after that. It was restructured and renamed to Connect2 in 2015/2016.

name: Nova Scotia Community College (NSCC) Enrolment

columns.name: International
columns.description: Integer variable which indicates the number of non-Canadian citizen/non-permanent resident students who paid international tuition fees.

columns.name: School
columns.description: String text variable which indicates the name of the school the program is operated by at NSCC. Categories include: Access, Education and Language; Business and Creative Industries; Health and Human Services; Technology and Environment; Trades and Transportation; School of Access; School of Business; School of Health & Human Services; School of IT & Creative Industries; and School of Trades & Technology.

columns.name: ProgramDescription
columns.description: String text variable which indicates the name of the college program intended to be used in combination with school to identify the area of study.

columns.name: Part-Time
columns.description: Integer variable which indicates the total number of students who were registered as part-time during the fall academic term.

columns.name: Nova Scotia
columns.description: Integer variable which indicates the total number of Canadian citizen/permanent residents students who described Nova Scotia as the province or place of residence at the student's time of application to NSCC.

columns.name: Academic Term
columns.description: String text variable indicates the academic term of the reporting, expressed as Fall-YYYY.

columns.name: Campus
columns.description: String text variable which indicates the name of the NSCC campus for which enrolment data correspond.

columns.name: Total Enrolment
columns.description: Numeric variable which indicates the total number of students enrolled at the milestone count as per campus, school, program description; expressed as a whole number. For 2010-2020 this count was done on September 30, for 2021 onward the count has been done on October 1. This column represents the total of all full-time (column F) and all part-time (column G) enrolments.

columns.name: Other CA Prov
columns.description: Integer variable which indicates the total number of Canadian citizen/permenant resident students who described any other province outside of Nova Scotia, as their province or place of residence at the time of application to NSCC.

columns.name: Full-Time
columns.description: Integer variable which indicates the total number of students who were registered as full-time during the fall academic term.

description: The following dataset contains total aggregate enrolment counts for the NSCC by fall term, campus, school, and program. name: 2019 Q1 Immigration Phone Survey Results
description: Data set is the result of commissioned questions in the Atlantic Quarterly omnibus public opinion survey conducted in February 2019. These commissioned questions relate to public opinion regarding perceptions of immigration. Results are provided for each question broken down by various demographic markers (age, gender, geographic region, education level and household income). Sample size consisted of 400 telephone interviews in a random sample of adult aged 18+ from Nova Scotia.

columns.name: recovery_date
columns.description: date the ADCP was retrieved.

columns.name: pings_per_ensemble
columns.description: number of measurements per ensemble. An entry of NA indicates this information was not recorded.

columns.name: sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m
columns.description: approximate distance between the ADCP transducer and the sea floor (metres).

columns.name: bin_size_m
columns.description: vertical size of parcels from which current data are recorded throughout the water column (metres).

columns.name: deployment_id
columns.description: unique identifier for ADCP deployment. IDs are assigned incrementally by deployment date within a county. A skipped ID (e.g., consecutive IDs are XY001 and XY003) indicates that an ADCP was deployed but no useable current data were recovered (e.g., the battery failed).

columns.name: waterbody
columns.description: waterbody in which the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: station
columns.description: specific area where ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: latitude
columns.description: approximate latitude where the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: longitude
columns.description: approximate longitude where the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: deployment_duration_days
columns.description: Number of days the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: sensor_model
columns.description: ADCP model used to record current data.

columns.name: sensor_serial_number
columns.description: serial number of the ADCP used to record the current data. An entry of NA indicates this information was not recorded.

columns.name: deployment_sounding_m
columns.description: depth sounding at the station prior to deployment (metres).

columns.name: first_bin_range_m
columns.description: distance between the sensing face of the instrument and the vertical center of the first recorded depth bin (metres).

columns.name: averaging_interval_s
columns.description: time span for averaging measurements (seconds). An entry of NA indicates this information was not recorded.

columns.name: county
columns.description: county where the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: lease
columns.description: Nova Scotia aquaculture lease within which ADCP was deployed. An entry of NA indicates the deployment was not associated with a lease.

columns.name: deployment_date
columns.description: date the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: ensemble_interval_s
columns.description: time span for one ensemble (group of averaged measurements; seconds). An entry of NA indicates this information was not recorded.

name: Nova Scotia Topographic Database - Structures (Point Layer)

Structure feature codes and their descriptions are provided with the download in a NSTDB feature code table. name: Nova Scotia Commissioner of Crown Lands
description: This is data of petitions made to government by individuals or groups of people seeking grants of Crown Land in early Nova Scotia, 1765-1800. name: New Arrivals at Adult and Youth Correctional Facilities
description: New arrivals in adult and youth facilities are counted as intakes only (e.g. from non‐custody status to custody, transfer from another jurisdiction, or conditional sentence to custody). An individual could have more than one new arrival at a correctional facility during a fiscal year. New arrivals do not include the transfer of individuals between correctional facilities within the province. Statistics presented are based on data sourced from the Justice Enterprise Information Network (JEIN).

name: Nova Scotia Assessment Results by Year and Board - Mathématiques en 8e année
description: Provides assessment results for Mathematics (scaled score and performance level) for each year assessment has been administered. Consult plans.ednet.ns.ca for more information on the assessments.

name: Bills introduced in the Nova Scotia Legislature

columns.name: Link
columns.description: URL for source data showing the progression of a bill through the legislature.

columns.name: 1st reading bill
columns.description: The first stage in the passage of a bill into law. The sponsoring member briefly introduces the bill. First reading always passes without debate or vote.

columns.name: 3rd reading bill
columns.description: The final stage in the consideration of a bill. Members have a chance to comment on, criticize, or ask questions about the bill again before voting on it for the last time.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: The current status of the bill.

columns.name: Assembly and Session
columns.description: After an election, the members form a new general assembly, which is numbered consecutively.  A session is a series of sittings of the House of Assembly during a General Assembly.

columns.name: Date Status Changed
columns.description: The date that the bill last changed its status.

columns.name: Title
columns.description: Short title of a bill, which is a proposed law to create an entirely new law or to change an existing one. To become law, a bill must pass three readings, committee study and be given Royal Assent.

columns.name: bill number
columns.description: The consecutive number the clerk assigns to a bill based upon when it is introduced in the House.

description: List of bills introduced in the Nova Scotia Legislature from 1995 to the present. Data set includes links to Status of Bills page and the bill as introduced. If the bill was passed by the legislature, there is also a link to the bill as assented to by the Lieutenant Governor.

It is an outcome of the Heritage Property Act.

name: NS Adult Admissions to Custody by Ethnicity

name: Honey Bee Winter Colony Losses

Data is compiled from a sample of commercial beekeepers (each keeping ≥50 colonies) each spring and presented as an estimated average percentage of colony losses.

columns.description: Number of bear caught per 100 snare nights expressed as a percentage, only applicable if harvest_method is Snaring.

columns.description: Number of snare nights equals number of snares multiplied by number of nights, only applicable if harvest_method is Snaring.

columns.description: Number of nights spent trapping, only applicable if harvest_method is Snaring.

columns.description: Hours spent hunting using a crossbow, only applicable if harvest_method is Hunting.

columns.description: Hours spent hunting using a bow, only applicable if harvest_method is Hunting.

columns.description: Total bears harvested as a result of this effort, for hunting maximum is one (1), for snaring maximum is two (2).

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county where the client spent time hunting or snaring for bear.

columns.description: This field will be either Hunting or Snaring.

description: This dataset is complementary to the Centre for Marine Applied Research’s (CMAR) Current Data and Wave Data County datasets. The Current and Wave data are measured by Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler (ADCPs), which are typically deployed for 1 – 3 months in a single location. This Deployment Information dataset provides details for each ADCP deployment, including the variable(s) measured, the deployment location, and instrument configuration.

columns.name: qc_flag_sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water speed (m/s). This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_sea_water_to_direction_degree
columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water direction. This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: sea_water_to_direction_degree

columns.name: sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: water speed for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (m / s).

columns.name: timestamp_utc
columns.description: timestamp of the start of the ensemble (in UTC). Current speed and direction recorded at each timestamp are an average of many measurements recoded during the ensemble. See the “Nova Scotia Current Data Deployment Information” dataset for ensemble details for each deployment.

columns.description: specific area where ADCP was deployed.

columns.description: unique identifier for ADCP deployment. IDs are assigned incrementally by deployment date within a county. A skipped ID (e.g., consecutive IDs are XY001 and XY003) indicates that an ADCP was deployed but no useable current data were recovered (e.g., the battery failed).

There are also bi-lateral meetings between Premiers and issue specific meetings (e.g. the Quebec Summit on Climate Change).

columns.name: string_configuration
columns.description: Configuration in which the sensors were deployed. This can be one of six entries: “sub-surface buoy”, “surface buoy”, “attached to gear”, “attached to fixed structure”, “floating dock”, or “unknown”

columns.name: waterbody
columns.description: Waterbody in which the station for this deployment is located

columns.name: station

columns.description: Latitude of the deployment (decimal degrees)

columns.description: String describing the instrument deployment and retrieval dates. The format is "YYYY-MMM-DD to YYYY-MMM-DD"

columns.description: Quality control flag for sensor depth at low tide. Flag values can be "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail". The same flag is applied to the whole deployment.

columns.name: qc_flag_dissolved_oxygen_percent_saturation
columns.description: Quality control flag for dissolved oxygen (percent saturation). Maximum flag value from four automated quality control tests: grossrange, climatology, spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_dissolved_oxygen_uncorrected_mg_per_l
columns.description: Quality control flag for dissolved oxygen (mg/L). Maximum flag value from four automated quality control tests: grossrange, climatology, spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_temperature_degree_c
columns.description: Quality control flag for temperature. Maximum flag value from three automated quality control tests: grossrange,  spike, and rolling_sd. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Not Evaluated", "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: salinity_psu

description: The Property Tax Rebate for Seniors helps low-income seniors with the cost of municipal residential property taxes. Rebates are 50% of what seniors paid on last year’s property taxes, up to a maximum of $800. Data field names: total applications received, applications approved, applications denied, average rebate amount, distribition rebate amount, opt-in applications to Heating Rebate Assistant Program.
