columns.name: sea_water_to_direction_degree
columns.description: direction of water flow for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (degrees). Measured travelling away from the ADCP sensor relative to True North (a reading of 90 degrees describes current travelling from west to east).

columns.name: sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: water speed for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (m / s).

columns.name: bin_height_above_sea_floor
columns.description: distance from the seafloor to the centre of the measurement bin (metres).

columns.name: bin_depth_below_surface
columns.description: distance from the surface to the centre of the measurement bin (metres). Calculated as sensor_depth_below_surface_m + sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m - bin_height_above_sea_floor_m. See “Nova Scotia Current Data: Deployment Information” dataset (https://data.novascotia.ca/d/rs2e-ekha) for sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m.

columns.name: timestamp_utc
columns.description: timestamp of the start of the ensemble (in UTC). Current speed and direction recorded at each timestamp are an average of many measurements recoded during the ensemble. See the “Nova Scotia Current Data Deployment Information” dataset for ensemble details for each deployment.

columns.name: station
columns.description: specific area where ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: deployment_ID
columns.description: unique identifier for ADCP deployment. IDs are assigned incrementally by deployment date within a county. A skipped ID (e.g., consecutive IDs are XY001 and XY003) indicates that an ADCP was deployed but no useable current data were recovered (e.g., the battery failed).

columns.name: sensor_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: the depth of the ADCP below the surface (metres), measured by the ADCP sensor. Changes with the tide.

columns.name: qc_flag_sensor_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: Quality control flag for sensor depth below surface. This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water speed (m/s). This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: waterbody
columns.description: waterbody in which the ADCP was deployed.

name: Family Pharmacare Program – Payment and Utilization by Age Group and Sex
description: The Nova Scotia Family Pharmacare Program is a provincial drug insurance plan designed to help Nova Scotians with the cost of their prescription drugs. The Program offers protection against drug costs for families who have no drug coverage or if the cost of the prescription drugs becomes a financial burden to them. The Program is available to all Nova Scotians with a valid Nova Scotia Health Card. This dataset lists the payment and utilization of the program by Age Group and Sex.

description: A geographic shape file of Nova Scotia’s Functional Economic Regions based on the 2021 census data. The most widely accepted approach to identifying functional economic regions is based on self-contained labour areas (SLAs) - a group of two or more Census Subdivisions (CSDs) where at least 75% of the workers both live and work in the area. Using Census data, Statistics Canada has identified Census Subdivisions (CSDs) that can be grouped together into SLAs based on daily commuting flows. The Offi

name: Inverness County Current Data

columns.name: qc_flag_sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water speed (m/s). This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: qc_flag_sea_water_to_direction_degree
columns.description: Quality control flag for sea water direction. This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: sea_water_speed_m_s
columns.description: water speed for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (m / s).

columns.name: station
columns.description: specific area where ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: waterbody
columns.description: waterbody in which the ADCP was deployed.

columns.name: deployment_id
columns.description: unique identifier for ADCP deployment. IDs are assigned incrementally by deployment date within a county. A skipped ID (e.g., consecutive IDs are XY001 and XY003) indicates that an ADCP was deployed but no useable current data were recovered (e.g., the battery failed).

columns.name: bin_height_above_sea_floor_m
columns.description: distance from the seafloor to the centre of the measurement bin (metres).

columns.name: bin_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: distance from the surface to the centre of the measurement bin (metres). Calculated as sensor_depth_below_surface_m + sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m - bin_height_above_sea_floor_m. See “Nova Scotia Current Data: Deployment Information” dataset (https://data.novascotia.ca/d/rs2e-ekha) for sensor_height_above_sea_floor_m.

columns.name: sensor_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: the depth of the ADCP below the surface (metres), measured by the ADCP sensor. Changes with the tide.

columns.name: timestamp_utc
columns.description: timestamp of the start of the ensemble (in UTC). Current speed and direction recorded at each timestamp are an average of many measurements recoded during the ensemble. See the “Nova Scotia Current Data Deployment Information” dataset for ensemble details for each deployment.

columns.name: qc_flag_sensor_depth_below_surface_m
columns.description: Quality control flag for sensor depth below surface. This is the maximum flag value from automated quality control tests. Flag values can be:  "Pass",  "Suspect/Of Interest", or "Fail"

columns.name: sea_water_to_direction_degree
columns.description: direction of water flow for corresponding depth bin and timestamp (degrees). Measured travelling away from the ADCP sensor relative to True North (a reading of 90 degrees describes current travelling from west to east).

columns.name: Opt-in Applications for Heating Assistance Rebate Program

description: The Property Tax Rebate for Seniors helps low-income seniors with the cost of municipal residential property taxes. Rebates are 50% of what seniors paid on last year’s property taxes, up to a maximum of $800. Data field names: total applications received, applications approved, applications denied, average rebate amount, distribution rebate amount, opt-in applications to Heating Rebate Assistant Program.

name: Family Pharmacare Program – Payment and Utilization
description: The Nova Scotia Family Pharmacare Program is a provincial drug insurance plan designed to help Nova Scotians with the cost of their prescription drugs. The Program offers protection against drug costs for families who have no drug coverage or if the cost of the prescription drugs becomes a financial burden to them. The Program is available to all Nova Scotians with a valid Nova Scotia Health Card. This dataset lists the summarized payment and utilization of the program for multiple fiscal years.

name: Residential SolarHomes Program Installations in Nova Scotia
description: Nova Scotia is a national leader in reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and the province remains committed to fighting climate change. Solar electricity provides clean and affordable renewable electricity. The SolarHomes Program began in 2018 and provides a rebate to Nova Scotian homeowners who install eligible solar projects.

name: Wetland Centre Education Programs Attendance
description: Shubenacadie Wildlife Park's Wetland Centre Education Programs Attendance displaying the total number of adults and childred that attended education programming per year.

name: Wetland Centre Education Programs Delivery
description: Shubenacadie Wildlife Park's Wetland Centre Education Programs displaying the total number of attendees and variety of programs offered and delivered each year.

name: Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) bloodstream infection rates in patients while in hospital
description: Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) bloodstream infection rates while in hospital are measured and monitored by infection prevention and control staff as a means to evaluate the effectiveness of their infection control protocols. MRSA rates are calculated by dividing the number of clostridium difficile infections by the total number of patient days. This rate is then multiplied by 10,000 to make a standard MRSA infection rate for each reporting period. The rate is reported per 10,

name: Atlantic Quarterly Spring 2018  Underground economy, tobacco and ecigarette use
description: Data set is results of commissioned questions the Atlantic Quaterly omnibus public opinion survey conducted between April 5 to August 5, 2018. The questions relate to public opinion and behaviour regarding “underground economy” (purchasing contraband tobacco) and tobacco and ecigarette use. Overall results are provided for each question and results are presented by demographic markers (age, gender, geographic region, education level and household income).

name: Communities, Culture, Tourism, and Heritage Funding Programs and Resources
description: The Department of Communities, Culture, Tourism and Heritage (CCTH) supports our communities to thrive bypromoting active living, tourism, and Nova Scotia’s diverse culture, heritage and languages. We pride ourselves in helping to grow communities and organizations by providing programs and services that support these areas.

name: Special Funding Assistance Program – Utilization and Payments
description: Special Funding Assistance Program – Utilization and Payments includes the following data fields: Year, Expenditures, Beneficiaries, Expenditures per Beneficiary

name: Utilization and Payments to the Nova Scotia Dental Surgical Program
description: Utilization and payments to the Nova Scotia Dental Surgical Program. The Nova Scotia Dental Surgical Program provides eligible residents of Nova Scotia coverage for medically necessary oral and maxillofacial surgery when hospital admission is required. Includes the following data fields: Fiscal Year, # of Services Rendered, Amount Paid, Persons Insured, Beneficiaries, # of Services per Insured Person, Paid per Insured Person, # of Services per Beneficiary, Paid per Beneficiary.

name: Utilization and Payments to the Nova Scotia Prosthetic Programs
description: The Nova Scotia Prosthetic Programs provides eligible residents of Nova Scotia coverage for the following programs: Breast Protheses, Limb Prostheses, and Ocular Prostheses. Includes the following data fields: Program, Fiscal Year, Services Rendered, Amount Paid, Beneficiaries, Paid Per Service, Services per Beneficiary, Paid per Beneficiary

name: Wildlife Compensation Claims by Commodity
description: The Wildlife Compensation Program provides compensation to producers who experience damage to specified agricultural products caused by specific wildlife species. Compensation compliments crop insurance plans that list wildlife as an insurable peril as well as operates on its own for crop and/or livestock farmers who do not currently have crop insurance. In all cases the maximum compensation payment is limited to 80% of the value of the agricultural products listed by the Crop and Animal Insuran

name: Healthcare Worker Influenza Immunization Rates
description: Healthcare worker influenza immunization rates represents the influenza immunization rates for healthcare workers within the Nova Scotia Health Authority and the IWK. The rates are collected and reported annually by the health authorities. Healthcare workers who get the flu vaccine provide a layer of protection to themselves and to patients from getting influenza and help prevent influenza outbreaks. name: Town Foundation Grant
description: Section 19A of the Municipal Grants Act provides that the Minister may pay each town a foundation grant. Each town receives an unconditional grant of $50,000 as recognition of the challenges they face due to local road responsibilities and assessment bases.

description: Enhanced death registrations 1970 data which resulted from Transcribe Nova Scotia, the Nova Scotia Archives’ collaborative online workspace where the public can participate in enhancing access to archival collections documenting over 300 years of Nova Scotia history, people, and culture.

name: Crop Insurance Operations by Year
description: Dataset indicates the total number of contracts by fiscal year, total coverage provided for all plans under the Crop and Animal Insurance Act, total premiums, as well as all claims paid within each fiscal year and the loss ratio which is the total claims paid/premiums collected. Total premiums include the producer, provincial, and federal cost shares.

name: Central Line Associated Bloodstream Infection (CLABSI) Rates in Intensive Care Units (ICU)
description: Central line associated bloodstream infection (CLABSI) rates are measured and monitored by infection prevention and control staff as a means to evaluate the effectiveness of their infection control protocols. CLABSI rates are calculated by dividing the number of central line bloodstream infections by the total number of days in which patients had a central line in the ICU in each reporting period. The rate is reported per 1,000 patient line days. The Canadian benchmark for each type of intensive

name: Clostridium difficile Infection (CDI) rates in patients while in hospital
description: Clostridium difficile Infection (CDI) rates while in hospital are measured and monitored by infection prevention and control staff as a means to evaluate the effectiveness of their infection control protocols. CDI rates are calculated by dividing the number of clostridium difficile infections by the total number of patient days. This rate is then multiplied by 10,000 to make a standard CDI infection rate for each reporting period. The rate is reported per 10,000 patient days. The Canadian benchm

name: Dairy Livestock Insurance Claims by Year
description: The annual summary of dairy livestock specifies the total claims paid out within a fiscal year against a loss of livestock resulting from one or more of the perils designated by the Dairy Livestock Insurance Plan Regulations under the Crop and Livestock Insurance Act.

name: Commodity Insurance Plans by Year
description: This dataset provides an annual update based on the fiscal year (April 1- March 31) of all the insurance plans outlined by regulations under the Crop and Livestock Insurance Act for the number of growers participating in the plan, number of acres, herds, trees or animals insured, total coverage in dollars for each plan, as well as total indemnities which provides protection against future loss or financial burden.

name: Family Pharmacare Payment and Utilization by Management Zone
description: The Nova Scotia Family Pharmacare Program is a provincial drug insurance plan designed to help Nova Scotians with the cost of their prescription drugs. The Program offers protection against drug costs for families who have no drug coverage or if the cost of the prescription drugs becomes a financial burden to them. The Program is available to all Nova Scotians with a valid Nova Scotia Health Card. This dataset lists the summary payment and utilization of the program by health management zone for

name: Dairy Livestock Insurance Plans by Year
description: Dataset indicates the total number of dairy livestock animals, total premiums collected and the claims paid for all perils within each fiscal year, and a loss ratio associated with each. The Loss ratio is determined by the claims paid/total premiums collected.

name: Poultry Livestock Insurance Plans by Type
description: The purpose of this plan is to provide for insurance against a loss of poultry resulting from the detection of a disease designated as a peril under the Poultry Insurance Plan regulations under the Crop and Livestock Insurance Act. This dataset indicates the number of poultry insured by weight or number of birds, number of claims within the fiscal year, amount paid and total coverage for each poultry type.

name: Poultry Livestock Insurance Plans by Year
description: Dataset indicates the total number of flocks, the number of insured birds, total premiums collected, claims paid, and total coverage for all poultry plan types within each fiscal year.

name: Lifeguard Beach Counts

columns.name: Count
columns.description: Lifeguard count of beachgoers.

columns.name: Beach
columns.description: Provincial park beach name.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Calendar Year.

description: Lifeguards in the provincial park beach program count beachgoers each day during the season.

name: Tourism Accommodations Real Property Tax Rebate Program (TARPTR)
description: The Tourism Accommodations Real Property Tax Rebate Program provides qualified operators a 25 per cent rebate on payment of 2020-21 commercial property tax. To be eligible, a business must meet the definition of a roofed accommodation as set out in the Tourist Accommodation Registration Act, be registered as a host under the act, have more than five rooms and have paid their tax bill in full.

name: Acute Myocardial Infarction (AMI) Crude Prevalence
description: The data set includes the calculated crude prevalence of acute myocardial infarction (AMI) aka heart attacks. It is segmented into age brackets for the each of the four health zones in the province.
